retry_reason: sock_put is a void function that unconditionally decrements the socket refcount. There is no failure case or condition to retry.
contract_summary: Batch put wrapper: releases ownership of a set of socket references stored in iter->batch[iter->cur_sk ... iter->end_sk). The function increments iter->cur_sk as it processes each socket. After this call, the caller must not use any of those socket pointers again, as their references have been released and the sockets may have been freed. This function is typically used to clean up the batch array after iterating over sockets in a BPF iterator. No return value or error handling is provided; all entries are assumed valid and eligible for sock_put.
